business.headline: Why Tier-II and Tier-III cities are driving India's next consumption boomFor years, India's consumer story centred on the metros. Today, smaller cities are rapidly taking the lead in spending, aspirations and premium consumption. Why are Tier-II and Tier-III cities booming?

Govt rules out using unclaimed LIC, EPFO funds for other purposesThe government told the Lok Sabha there is no proposal to divert unclaimed LIC funds or EPFO inoperative account money. It said the existing transfer and settlement mechanisms will continue while outreach to claimants is stepped up.

US-India trade deal likely in 3-4 months, says senior US officialA senior US official has said that negotiations between the two countries are almost complete, with only a few procedural steps remaining before the deal can be finalised.

Middle East conflict could drag global growth to 1.3% in 2026, warns World BankAccording to World Bank Chief Economist Indermit Gill, if hostilities continue for several more months, the world could face slower growth, higher inflation and rising borrowing costs.

Sensex ends 715 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 4%The BSE Sensex fell 715.06 points, or 0.92%, to close at 76,755.05, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 191.45 points, or 0.79%, to settle at 23,996.25, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.
Can you settle pending PF damages at lower rates? This EPFO scheme can helpOne of the biggest attractions of the Vishwas 2026 Scheme is the reduced rate of penal damages.

India may open skies to new airlines in bid to challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyIndia is considering easing rules to let airport operators such as Adani and GMR run airlines. The move is aimed at widening competition in a domestic market dominated by IndiGo and Air India.

Property tax rules, AIS errors and more: 6 biggest ITR filing challenges this yearITR filing may be digital and largely pre-filled, but taxpayers are still running into several roadblocks. I spoke to tax experts to find out where taxpayers are making the most mistakes this filing season. Are you making one of them too?

Why are your foreign bank accounts and investments showing in AIS?Did you log into your Annual Information Statement (AIS) and notice your foreign bank account or overseas investments listed there? Wondering how the Income Tax Department got those details and what it means for your ITR filing?

Lupin, Aurobindo, Sun Pharma fall up to 4%: Why are pharma stocks falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply after Donald Trump announced a phased tariff plan for imported generic medicines. The proposal has raised concerns over costs, manufacturing shifts and the long-term outlook for Indian drugmakers.

Sensex tanks 700 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayBenchmark indices extended their losses in afternoon trade, with the BSE Sensex tumbling 746.20 points, or 0.96%, to 76,723.91, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 198.55 points, or 0.82%, to slip below the key 24,000 mark and trade at 23,989.15 around 12:24 pm.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Brent crude climbs above $92The BSE Sensex fell 367.92 points, or 0.47%, to 77,102.19, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 99.10 points, or 0.41%, to 24,088.60 at around 9:20 am.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall as crude crosses $92 mark?GIFT Nifty futures were trading around 24,117 at 7:56 am IST, indicating that the Nifty50 could open below Tuesday's close of 24,187.70.

Are you eligible for additional UPS benefits? Here's what govt saysReplying to a question in the Lok Sabha, Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary said that 25,756 retired Central government subscribers are eligible to claim additional benefits under the UPS.

Volkswagen weighs JSW partnership for India businessVolkswagen is in advanced talks with JSW Group over an investment in Skoda Auto Volkswagen India. The proposed deal could reshape the carmaker's India strategy as competition intensifies.

Everyone wants AI engineers. But who will make sure AI actually works?As companies increasingly deploy AI across banking, healthcare, customer service and other sectors, another talent shortage may be quietly emerging; professionals who can test whether these systems are accurate, secure and reliable before they reach users.

Maruti Suzuki announces fresh price hike: Check how much more you'll have to payMaruti Suzuki will raise vehicle prices by up to Rs 30,000 from August. The move marks its second portfolio-wide increase in about two months amid sustained cost pressures.

Can ITR portal handle the filing rush before July 31? Here's what government saysIn a written reply in Parliament, the government said that while some users may experience occasional delays during periods of heavy traffic, the portal has remained largely stable despite a significant increase in the number of taxpayers using it this year.

RBI's foreign currency deposit scheme for NRIs explainedThe RBI has rolled out a concessional swap facility to revive FCNR(B) deposits after a sharp fall in inflows. The move could help banks raise rates, but NRIs still need to weigh currency needs and taxes.

Dalal Street ends lower on HDFC Bank drag; midcaps continue to outperformThe BSE Sensex closed 238.41 points, or 0.31%, lower at 77,470.11, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 50.80 points, or 0.21%, to settle at 24,187.70.

entertainment.headline: Dulquer Salmaan, Pooja Bhatt to Imtiaz Ali: Celebs rally behind CJP protest More film and music personalities have backed students protesting over the alleged NEET paper leak and the crackdown on demonstrators. Their posts have called for empathy, accountability and peaceful dialogue as protests continue.

Paper leak a serious issue, students showed courage: Salman Khan backs CJP protest Salman Khan has expressed his support for the CJP protest, commending students for their peaceful demand for educational reforms while cautioning against politicisation.

Aamir Khan's ex-sister-in-law says actor's brother Hyder Ali Khan has schizophrenia Eva Grover has spoken about her marriage to Hyder Ali Khan, alleging years of physical abuse amid his schizophrenia. She said the relationship ended in divorce, a long custody separation from her daughter and lasting concern over his present condition.

The End of Oak Street trailer: Anne Hathaway's suburb turns dinosaur battleground Warner Bros has released a new trailer and posters for The End of Oak Street, starring Anne Hathaway and Ewan McGregor. The footage stresses the film's core hook as the Platt family battles dinosaurs and tries to stay together after a cosmic event upends their neighbourhood.

Shraddha Kapoor hunts for new lead as Aamir Khan steps out of Ashneer Grover biopic Actor Aamir Khan has reportedly exited the Ashneer Grover biopic after creative differences with Rahul Mody. The film also featured actor-turned-producer Shraddha Kapoor in a key role.

Meet Devyn Dalton, Matt Damon's 34-year-old female stunt double in The Odyssey Matt Damon said his muscular look in The Odyssey did not belong to him alone, crediting stunt performer Devyn Dalton for key shots in the Laestrygonians sequence. 34-year-old Dalton is a Canadian artist with a promising body of work.

Disha Patani's sister Khushboo faces lathi-charge at CJP's Chalo Sansad march Actor Disha Patani's sister Khushboo said she was caught in a lathi-charge during a Delhi protest demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. In a video message, she described the crowd as peaceful and said women felt safe despite the violence.

Dhurandhar actor Ayesha Khan detained by Mumbai Police during protest. Watch Actor Ayesha Khan was detained by Mumbai Police during a peaceful protest in support of NEET aspirants, highlighting concerns over examination irregularities and student suicides. The Dhurandhar star shared her ordeal on social media, questioning the police action despite their group not actively protesting.

Why Shabana Azmi's critics keep forgetting her four decades of activism Shabana Azmi joined students during the CJP's Chalo Sansad march in Delhi on July 20. Her presence drew attention because it echoed a decades-long record of public activism.

My heart is crying: Actor Dibyendu Bhattacharya backs student protesters in Delhi Actor Dibyendu Bhattacharya backed the CJP's student-led protest and condemned the police action during the Chalo Sansad march. He said the movement was about the future of students and called for wider support for education reforms.

Government blocks 50 OTT platforms in 2 years over obscene content The Centre has disabled 50 OTT platforms in India over the past two years after complaints over obscene content. The action was taken under the IT Rules, 2021 and other laws covering unlawful content and indecent representation.

The Legend of Karna trailer: An animated saga of the Mahabharata warrior The upcoming original animated series The Legend of Karna is set to unravel the story of one of the most valiant Mahabharata warriors. The show places Karna's loyalty, lineage and struggle with destiny at its centre.

ZEE5 announces series on NEET paper leak amid students' protest at Jantar Mantar An OTT series titled The Scam: Leaked has been announced as protests over examination irregularities widen. Its teaser arrives amid student unrest, celebrity support and calls for education reforms.

Anupam Kher backs students, warns against political opportunists hijacking protest Anupam Kher backed students protesting over the NEET paper leak and education reform. He urged them to keep the movement free of political opportunists as the Jantar Mantar protest continued.

Jana Nayagan producer upset over A certificate, cites girl child empowerment theme Producer Venkat K Narayana said Jana Nayagan's A certificate was a disappointment after the makers hoped for a U/A rating. He said the decision limits the film's reach among families despite its message on girl child empowerment.

I wanted Punjab to feel lived-in, not an outsider's view: Ab Hisaab Hoga director Ab Hisaab Hoga director Divyanshu said he worked to make Punjab feel authentic in the series. He said the show's workshops and rooted details reflect why OTT should keep backing smaller stories.

R Madhavan to host OTT reboot of Farooq Shaikh's talk show Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai Actor R Madhavan will host the OTT return of Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai, taking over from late actor Farooq Shaikh. Madhavan said the reboot will honour the show's legacy of empathy, sincerity and personal storytelling.

Can Jana Nayagan beat the leak? Trade predicts Vijay's box office fate Trade voices are divided over CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan's opening after its leak and delayed release. Strong advance bookings continue, but piracy and the A certificate may affect repeat footfalls.

Ryan Reynolds confirms return as Wade Wilson in new Deadpool film Actor Ryan Reynolds confirmed another Deadpool film is in the works at the Fanatics Fest 2026. Reynolds had last featured alongside actor Hugh Jackman in Marvel Studios' Deadpool & Wolverine.

general news.headline: CJP protest turns violent again, 5 cops injured as miscreants throw stones at policeOfficials said Delhi Police teams were stationed at the designated protest site as part of routine security arrangements when the protesters suddenly turned aggressive and launched the attack. The RAF and police personnel then resorted to limited force and fired two to three tear gas shells before clearing the area.

Live: Govt reviews security as CJP protests spread across states, say sourcesCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: Protesters of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) reclaimed Delhi's Jantar Mantar on Tuesday morning after police dismantled the protest stage and tents overnight following a day of clashes between demonstrators and security personnel. Heavy deployment of Delhi Police and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel remained in place around the protest site. Leader of the Opposition Rahul Gandhi, whose absence from the protest had drawn attention as several opposition leaders and prominent citizens thronged the site, later led a group of Congress MPs in a sit-in outside the Prime Minister's residence. This led to dramatic scenes as police removed Gandhi and other lawmakers from the area. Gandhi was briefly detained before being released. Tensions escalated again late Tuesday when stone-pelting was reported on Delhi Police and RAF personnel in Connaught Place, a short distance from Jantar Mantar. Stay with Indiatoday.in for latest updates

No oil sales in the region if Tehran blocked from exports, says Iran's QalibafIranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf delivered the warning in a post on X. He said the conflict had reached a stage where the entire region's energy exports could be put at risk.

Paper leak a serious issue, students showed courage: Salman Khan backs CJP protestSalman Khan has expressed his support for the CJP protest, commending students for their peaceful demand for educational reforms while cautioning against politicisation.

Congratulations, we have a functioning democracy!From the government to the opposition to the protesters and the police, and not to forget the algorithm, everyone has played their role. The democracy is alive, and kicking.
TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee suspended from Lok Sabha over remarks against women MPsKalyan Banerjee was accused of using "unsavoury" language against women MPs during a heated exchange with NCPI members.

Who's feeding the protest? At Jantar Mantar, meals keep arriving from strangersAfter the July 20 crackdown on the CJP protest, something unexpected happened. As questions over the protest's funding grew louder, anonymous supporters began sending food to Jantar Mantar in such large quantities that volunteers say they now have more than they need.

Dharmendra Pradhan's daughter quits Instagram after foreign degree backlashNaimisha Pradhan deactivated her Instagram account after users targeted her over studying abroad while her father faces criticism over examination issues. The episode has widened the row into a debate over online abuse of relatives who hold no public office.

Did Dharmendra Pradhan lead a 1997 paper leak protest?Protests over alleged exam irregularities have once again put the spotlight on the Union Education Minister, social media has unearthed an old claim from his student politics days. A 2021 article recounts his participation in a 1997 protest linked to a paper leak...

Watch: Drunk man steals Telangana bus, triggers 18-km police chaseDuring the pursuit, the accused allegedly drove recklessly, ramming into three two-wheelers and a police vehicle, triggering panic among commuters and motorists on the road.

I am still alive: Sonam Wangchuk's first video message from Gurugram hospitalActivist Sonam Wangchuk released a video message from Gurugram's Medanta Hospital on the 25th day of his fast, saying he was prepared to end it if the government guaranteed that students who took part in Monday's march would not face police action, arrests or harassment.

Who are India's top medal contenders in Commonwealth Games?India have sent a 126-member strong squad for the Commonwealth Games 2026 in Glasgow. Who are India's favourites to medal in the competition? Let's have a look.

US will attack Iran's bridge, power plant for every ship attacked in Hormuz: TrumpDonald Trump said the US would respond to any Iranian attack on commercial or military vessels in Strait of Hormuz, regardless of the type of weapon used.

Rajasthan High Court allows two life convicts to marry inside Jodhpur prisonTwo life convicts married at Jodhpur's Mandore open jail after the Rajasthan High Court allowed the ceremony. The court said consenting prisoners retain the right to marry and such unions aid rehabilitation.

Indian-origin couple convicted in UK COVID fraud; blew millions on Porsche, RolexesAn Indian-origin couple and their associate were convicted in the UK over fraudulent Covid-19 PPE deals. Investigators said the money was diverted into luxury spending while promised glove shipments never arrived.

Skyroot CEO recalls phone call that brought father to tears after Vikram-1 successAfter Vikram-1 reached orbit, Skyroot Aerospace CEO Pawan Kumar Chandana said his first call was to his father. Recalling his reaction, Chandana said the conversation was filled with silence and emotion.

IPL founder Lalit Modi set to return to India after 16 yearsIPL founder and former chairman Lalit Modi has said he will return to India after 16 years following a tribunal verdict that gave him major relief in the 2009 IPL South Africa Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) case.

Godzilla El Nino should mean less monsoon rain, so why is 95% of India cloudy?A Godzilla-strength El Nino should mean a weak monsoon, yet 95 per cent of India is under cloud cover this week. Here is why the monsoon trough, the Bay of Bengal and the Indian Ocean Dipole can still overrule the Pacific.

Govt ready for in-depth Parliament discussion on paper leaks: JP NaddaNadda's briefing came hours after Leader of Opposition and Congress MP Rahul Gandhi, in a media briefing, claimed that 152 examination paper leaks have taken place over the past 10 years without a single conviction.

Chilling CCTV visuals show terrorist shooting J&K cop at point-blank rangeA suspected terrorist shot constable Aashiq Hussain Qureshi at close range near Lal Chowk in Anantnag. The daylight killing triggered panic in the market and marked a major terror incident in the Valley since the Pahalgam attack last year.

Why Rahul Gandhi launched a protest separate from the CJP'sCongress leader Rahul Gandhi didn't join the Cockroach Janta Party's protest at Jantar Mantar. Instead, he started one of his own. Here's why India's main opposition party chose a parallel agitation instead of standing with the student-led movement already on the streets.

Indian-origin doctor jailed in US for secretly filming staff in hospital restroomA former Indian-origin medical resident in Ohio was jailed for secretly recording women in a hospital staff restroom. The court also ordered long-term sex offender registration, probation and continued treatment.

Did Delhi Police use excessive force? HC wants Parliament march videos preservedAmid allegations that excessive force was used to deter protesters marching to Parliament on July 20, the Delhi High Court has ordered the Delhi Police to preserve all video evidence from that day.

Sarkar hataiye, cops whispered as they dragged me away, quips Rahul GandhiRahul Gandhi quipped that when he was being dragged away from outside the Prime Minister's residence, the police personnel privately expressed discontent with the BJP government. Rahul shared the anecdote during a press conference on Wednesday.

sports.headline: Who are India's top medal contenders in Commonwealth Games? India have sent a 126-member strong squad for the Commonwealth Games 2026 in Glasgow. Who are India's favourites to medal in the competition? Let's have a look.

When Novak Djokovic backed Serbia's student protest movement As student protests gather pace in New Delhi, Novak Djokovic's support for Serbia's student movement has come back into focus. The 24-time Grand Slam champion has stood by Serbian students since nationwide protests began after the deadly Novi Sad railway station tragedy in November 2024.

Alireza Firouzja clinches Chennai Grand Masters title after draw vs Arjun French Grandmaster Alireza Firouzja won the fourth edition of the Chennai Grand Masters after drawing with India's Arjun Erigaisi in the seventh and final round on Wednesday.

Shreyas Iyer wants fearless India to bounce back in Zimbabwe after rough UK tour India captain Shreyas Iyer wants his players to play fearless cricket and ignore outside criticism after India's disappointing tour of England. He believes the difficult phase has brought the squad closer ahead of the three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe.

Rahmat Shah to lead Afghanistan in Tests and ODIs, Gurbaz becomes vice-captain The Afghanistan Cricket Board (ACB) has appointed experienced batter Rahmat Shah as the new captain of its Test and One-Day International (ODI) teams following the resignation of Hashmatullah Shahidi.

IPL founder Lalit Modi set to return to India after 16 years IPL founder and former chairman Lalit Modi has said he will return to India after 16 years following a tribunal verdict that gave him major relief in the 2009 IPL South Africa Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) case.

Sindhu wildly celebrates win vs Unnati Hooda, shares cryptic social media post China Open 2026: A huge roar on court, followed by a cryptic message off it. PV Sindhu battled past Unnati Hooda in three games before sharing a social media post that sparked plenty of intrigue.

UTT: Kolkata edge U Mumba in thriller as both teams seal semis spot Kolkata Thunder Blades ended defending champions U Mumba TT's winning streak with a dramatic 8-7 victory to secure a place in the UTT Season 7 semi-finals, with both teams advancing to the knockout stage.

Benched in England, Kuldeep Yadav signs for Yorkshire for one-dayers and County Kuldeep Yadav has joined Yorkshire after being left out of India's ODI XI in England. The stint gives him immediate match time before the Sri Lanka Tests and a county run with the English club later in the year.

Judge us after: Fulton, tactical discipline, and India's quest to exorcise 1975 For Craig Fulton, the aim at the World Cup is to end a 51-year-old semi-final curse for the Indian team. With this goal firmly in his mind, the Indian coach has decided to go for a team that will fit into his mantra of tactical discipline, structure and long-term stability.

ICC ODI rankings: Rohit Sharma closes on Virat Kohli as No. 1 battle heats up Rohit Sharma gained 14 rating points after his hundred at Lord's on Saturday. Three of the top four in the ICC rankings for men's ODIs are Indians. Shubman Gill is closing on in No. 1 Daryl Mitchell.

Ben Stokes battles cramps, scores first hundred after international retirementBen Stokes returned for Durham and made an unbeaten 108 against Derbyshire despite severe cramps. The innings underlined his resilience and gave Durham a five-wicket win days after his Test retirement call.

Embracing the rollercoaster, Sooryavanshi returns to favourite hunting groundZimbabwe vs India:Vaibhav Sooryavanshi returns to Harare for India's Zimbabwe T20Is after a testing debut in England. The 15-year-old hopes familiar conditions help convert promise into a first senior impact.

Scaloni rejects unfair refereeing chat: Spain were better in World Cup finalLionel Scaloni rejected complaints about the refereeing after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain. He said Spain were the better side, while admitting that fatigue may have also been a reason behind the loss.

Hasmatullah Shahidi steps down as Afghanistan ODI captain historic 4-year runHashmatullah Shahidi has stepped down as Afghanistan's ODI captain but remains available for selection. His exit closes a notable phase that included a near semi-final World Cup run in 2023 and opens the search for a new skipper before the Ireland series.

David Warner pleads guilty to drink driving, faces up to 9 months in jailDavid Warner pleaded guilty to a mid-range drink-driving charge in Sydney through his lawyer. The conviction could bring jail time and trigger a review of his Sydney Thunder captaincy.

Rohit Sharma proves Rohit Sharma isn't India's ODI problemRohit Sharma answered the only question worth asking of him at Lord's. What India actually need answered are the harder ones: a bowling attack built around one man's fitness, a potential lead spinner being overlooked, and search for all-rounders without a plan.

Did FIFA let Argentina's Leandro Paredes off the hook after World Cup final brawl?Leandro Paredes' red card after the brawl following the World Cup final has been deleted by FIFA. Paredes was seen coming to blows with Eric Garcia and Gavi after the end of the game.

Chelsea take sly dig at Arsenal after completing record Morgan Rogers dealChelsea completed a record British deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa after beating Arsenal to him. The Blues decided to a cheeky dig at their London rivals after Rogers picked them over the current Premier League champions.

Think India will bludgeon past Zimbabwe in the T20I series? Think againZIM vs IND: India may be the more experienced side heading into the T20I series, but Zimbabwe can be a dangerous proposition at home and should not be underestimated.

Tilak Varma gives perfect response to strike-rate critics. Here's what he saidTilak Varma pushed back against questions over his reduced strike-rate before the Zimbabwe series. Tilak has struggled to maintain a consistent strike-rate over the last couple of years in T20Is.

Fresh off Lord's heroics, Rohit Sharma arrives in style for The Hundred openerRohit Sharma turned up at the Oval for The Hundred opener after his fighting 138 at Lord's and was seen with fans before an intense chat with Nita Ambani. The visit for the match came on the heels of his hundred against England, which effectively shut down rumours of retirement.

La Liga chief accuses FIFA of destroying football, calls for Infantino to resignLa Liga president Javier Tebas accused FIFA of "destroying the football industry" through the expansion of international competitions and said president Gianni Infantino should resign.

India boxing team hit by luggage chaos ahead of Commonwealth Games campaignIndia's boxing contingent suffered an early setback to its Commonwealth Games campaign on Tuesday after nearly half the team's luggage failed to arrive from Belfast.

Football's strangest red card? Player sent-off for urinating next to benchBrazilian forward Micael was shown a rare red card before kick-off in the Copa Paulista after urinating beside the substitutes' bench. However, the team did not have to play with a man short.

Chennai Grand Masters: D Gukesh suffers second loss, Sarin beats ErigaiseWorld champion D Gukesh suffered his second consecutive defeat at the Chennai Grand Masters after losing to GM M Pranesh, while GM Nihal Sarin stunned Arjun Erigaisi in the penultimate round on Tuesday.

When in doubt, talk to Ashish Nehra: Ashok Sharma ahead of potential India debutIndia fast bowler Ashok Sharma credited Gujarat Titans head coach Ashish Nehra for his rapid rise, revealing the former India pacer was always available for advice as he prepares for a likely T20I debut against Zimbabwe.

Rohit Sharma's retirement speculation wasn't merely outside noise: AshwinFormer India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin believes there was substance to the speculation surrounding Rohit Sharma's ODI future. Speaking on his YouTube channel, Ashwin said that there is no smoke without fire.

Pressure is intense: Referee Anthony Taylor retires from football officiatingReferee Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from football officiating with immediate effect, saying the intense pressure and constant scrutiny of the job played a key role in his decision.

Murali Sreeshankar eyes Glasgow gold after strong injury comebackIndia's premier long jumper Murali Sreeshankar believes his finest performances are still ahead of him as he prepares for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, saying a "big jump" is around the corner after making an impressive return from a career-threatening injury.

India's recent T20I slump due to overplaying in Asia, says Tilak VarmaVice-captain Tilak Varma addressed India's recent T20I slump during the pre-series press conference in Harare. Varma argued that the team lost all six T20Is in the UK because they spent the majority of the last two years playing in Asia.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
